Activity: Provided tutorial support for first-year undergraduate psychology students in R programming, data wrangling, data visualisation, and statistical analysis (correlations, t-tests, chi-squared tests, and linear regression).

Position: Doctoral Tutor for Quantitative and Qualitative Methods
Activity: Facilitated students’ critical engagement with quantitative and qualitative research methods in psychology, with a particular focus on measurement issues and the interpretive decisions researchers make when investigating complex psychological constructs.
Supported students in developing both theoretical understanding and practical skills in implementing, interpreting, and reporting statistical analyses, with a focus on the linear model.
